cedar.explanation: Cedar evaluates each (principal, action) request in isolation. It cannot detect capability emergence from agent composition. This is not a Cedar bug — it is a structural limitation of the per-request policy paradigm. See: CedarBridge.lean, theorem cedar_nonCompositionality_gap.

theorems_cited.name: nonCompositionality_universal
theorems_cited.statement: ∀ edges a b, isCapSafe edges a = true → isCapSafe edges b = true → (∃ e ∈ edges, …) → isCapSafe edges (compose a b) = false
theorems_cited.english: For any agents and hyperedges satisfying the structural premises, individual safety does not survive composition.

specs.name: web-search-agent
specs.description: Extracted from web_search_agent.py via AST analysis

specs.description: Extracted from code_exec_agent.py via AST analysis

AgentProgram.lean.name: bindSafe
AgentProgram.lean.statement: (contract : Agent) (x : Prog α) (f : α → Prog β) (hx : SafeProg contract x) (hf : ∀ a, SafeProg contract (f a)) : SafeProg contract (x.bind f)

AgentProgram.lean.name: searchProg_safe
AgentProgram.lean.docstring: An unsafe program: searches then exfiltrates the result

AgentProgram.lean.name: exfilProg_unsafe
AgentProgram.lean.docstring: The exfil program is unsafe: exfilData is forbidden for webAgent

AgentProgram.lean.name: safeProg_caps_in_base
AgentProgram.lean.statement: (contract : Agent) (e : AgentEffect β) (k : β → Prog α) (h : SafeProg contract (.step e k)) : effectCap e ∈ contract.base ∧ effectCap e ∉ contract.forbidden
AgentProgram.lean.docstring:
STEP-LEVEL SOUNDNESS: every effect in a SafeProg-safe program
    uses only capabilities that are in the contract's base and
    not in the contract's forbidden set.

    This is the key per-step guarantee: SafeProg checks exactly this
    at every node of the program tree.

AgentProgram.lean.name: safeProg_base_forbidden_disjoint
AgentProgram.lean.statement: (contract : Agent) (e : AgentEffect β) (k : β → Prog α) (h : SafeProg contract (.step e k)) : effectCap e ∈ contract.base \ contract.forbidden

AgentProgram.lean.name: safeProg_implies_capSafe_empty_edges
AgentProgram.lean.statement: (contract : Agent) (prog : Prog α) (_hSafe : SafeProg contract prog) (hDisjoint : contract.base ∩ contract.forbidden = ∅) : isCapSafe [] contract = true

AgentProgram.lean.name: safeProg_does_not_imply_capSafe
AgentProgram.lean.statement: : -- A program can be SafeProg-safe: SafeProg webAgent searchProg ∧ -- While a related contract is declaratively unsafe: isCapSafe demoEdges (compose webAgent execAgent) = false ∧ -- And the individual agent is safe under its own contract: isCapSafe demoEdges webAgent = true

AgentProgram.lean.name: safeProg_continuation_safe
AgentProgram.lean.statement: (contract : Agent) (e : AgentEffect β) (k : β → Prog α) (h : SafeProg contract (.step e k)) : ∀ b, SafeProg contract (k b)
AgentProgram.lean.docstring:
CONTINUATION SAFETY: every continuation of a SafeProg-safe step
    is itself SafeProg-safe. This is the inductive structure of
    SafeProg made explicit — safety is preserved through all
    execution paths.

AgentProgram.lean.name: safeProg_bind_compositional
AgentProgram.lean.statement: (contract : Agent) (x : Prog α) (f : α → Prog β) (hx : SafeProg contract x) (hf : ∀ a, SafeProg contract (f a)) : SafeProg contract (x.bind f)

CapHypergraph.lean.name: step_mono
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: (edges : List HyperEdge) {S T : Finset Cap} (h : S ⊆ T) : step edges S ⊆ step edges T
CapHypergraph.lean.docstring: `step` is monotone: S ⊆ T → step edges S ⊆ step edges T

CapHypergraph.lean.name: closureAux_mono
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: (n : Nat) (edges : List HyperEdge) {S T : Finset Cap} (h : S ⊆ T) : closureAux n edges S ⊆ closureAux n edges T
CapHypergraph.lean.docstring: `closureAux` is monotone in its Finset argument.

CapHypergraph.lean.name: capClosure_mono
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: (edges : List HyperEdge) {S T : Finset Cap} (h : S ⊆ T) : capClosure edges S ⊆ capClosure edges T
CapHypergraph.lean.docstring:
The closure operator is monotone:
    a larger base set always yields a larger emergent set.

CapHypergraph.lean.name: capClosure_extensive
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: (edges : List HyperEdge) (S : Finset Cap) : S ⊆ capClosure edges S
CapHypergraph.lean.docstring: The closure is extensive: every base capability survives into the closure.

CapHypergraph.lean.name: closureAux_empty
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: (n : Nat) (S : Finset Cap) : closureAux n [] S = S
CapHypergraph.lean.docstring: With no hyperedges, `step` is the identity.

CapHypergraph.lean.name: capClosure_empty
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: (S : Finset Cap) : capClosure [] S = S
CapHypergraph.lean.docstring:
With no hyperedges, `capClosure` is the identity:
    no capability emergence is possible without hyperedges.

CapHypergraph.lean.name: closureAux_step_subset
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: (n : Nat) (edges : List HyperEdge) (S : Finset Cap) : closureAux n edges S ⊆ closureAux (n + 1) edges S
CapHypergraph.lean.docstring:
`closureAux` is non-decreasing in its iteration count:
    one more application of `step` always yields a superset.

CapHypergraph.lean.name: capClosure_is_fixpoint
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: (edges : List HyperEdge) (S : Finset Cap) : step edges (capClosure edges S) = capClosure edges S
CapHypergraph.lean.docstring: `capClosure` is an absolute fixed point under `step`.

CapHypergraph.lean.name: capClosure_is_least_fixpoint
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: (edges : List HyperEdge) (S T : Finset Cap) (hS : S ⊆ T) (hfix : step edges T ⊆ T) : capClosure edges S ⊆ T
CapHypergraph.lean.docstring:
`capClosure` is the LEAST closed superset of `S`:
    any set T that contains S and is closed under all hyperedges
    must contain the closure.
    This is the minimal fixed-point property — `capClosure` is not merely
    *a* closed superset, but the *smallest possible* one.

CapHypergraph.lean.name: webAgent_is_safe
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: : isCapSafe demoEdges webAgent = true

CapHypergraph.lean.name: execAgent_is_safe
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: : isCapSafe demoEdges execAgent = true

CapHypergraph.lean.name: composedTeam_is_unsafe
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: : isCapSafe demoEdges (compose webAgent execAgent) = false

CapHypergraph.lean.name: nonCompositionalityCounterexample
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: : isCapSafe demoEdges webAgent = true ∧ isCapSafe demoEdges execAgent = true ∧ isCapSafe demoEdges (compose webAgent execAgent) = false
CapHypergraph.lean.docstring:
The headline theorem: both agents are individually safe,
    yet their composition is unsafe — safety is non-compositional.

CapHypergraph.lean.name: nonCompositionality_exists
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: : ∃ (edges : List HyperEdge) (a b : Agent), isCapSafe edges a = true ∧ isCapSafe edges b = true ∧ isCapSafe edges (compose a b) = false
CapHypergraph.lean.docstring:
There *exist* agents and hyperedges such that individual safety
    does not imply compositional safety.
    This upgrades our counterexample from a verified instance to an
    existential theorem — the correct research-paper-level statement.

CapHypergraph.lean.name: nonCompositionality_universal
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: : ∀ (edges : List HyperEdge) (a b : Agent), isCapSafe edges a = true → isCapSafe edges b = true → (∃ e ∈ edges, ¬ (e.premises ⊆ a.base) ∧ ¬ (e.premises ⊆ b.base) ∧ e.premises ⊆ a.base ∪ b.base ∧ e.conclusion ∈ a.forbidden ∪ b.forbidden) → isCapSafe edges (compose a b) = false

CapHypergraph.lean.name: safeRegion_downward_closed
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: (edges : List HyperEdge) (forbidden : Finset Cap) (A B : Finset Cap) (hSafe : (capClosure edges A ∩ forbidden).card = 0) (hSub : B ⊆ A) : (capClosure edges B ∩ forbidden).card = 0
CapHypergraph.lean.docstring:
DOWNWARD CLOSURE: a smaller base capability set is always at least as safe.
    If starting from A is safe, starting from any subset B ⊆ A is also safe.
    This is because `capClosure` is monotone: fewer starting capabilities
    can only produce fewer emergent capabilities.

CapHypergraph.lean.name: safeRegion_closed_under_intersection
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: (edges : List HyperEdge) (forbidden : Finset Cap) (A B : Finset Cap) (hA : (capClosure edges A ∩ forbidden).card = 0) (_hB : (capClosure edges B ∩ forbidden).card = 0) : (capClosure edges (A ∩ B) ∩ forbidden).card = 0

CapHypergraph.lean.name: compose_forbidden_union_justified
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: (edges : List HyperEdge) (a b : Agent) (h : isCapSafe edges (compose a b) = true) : ∀ cap ∈ a.forbidden ∪ b.forbidden, cap ∉ capClosure edges (a.base ∪ b.base)

CapHypergraph.lean.name: agent_base_subset_coalition
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: (agents : List Agent) (a : Agent) (ha : a ∈ agents) : a.base ⊆ (composeList agents).base

CapHypergraph.lean.name: agent_forbidden_subset_coalition
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: (agents : List Agent) (a : Agent) (ha : a ∈ agents) : a.forbidden ⊆ (composeList agents).forbidden

CapHypergraph.lean.name: coalition_closure_dominates_individual
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: (edges : List HyperEdge) (agents : List Agent) (a : Agent) (ha : a ∈ agents) : emergent edges a ⊆ coalitionEmergent edges agents

CapHypergraph.lean.name: coalition_nonCompositionality
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: : (∀ a ∈ ([webAgent, execAgent] : List Agent), isCapSafe demoEdges a = true) ∧ isCapSafe demoEdges (composeList [webAgent, execAgent]) = false

CapHypergraph.lean.name: coalition_unsafe_from_dangerous_pair
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: (edges : List HyperEdge) (agents : List Agent) (a b : Agent) (ha : a ∈ agents) (hb : b ∈ agents) (_haSafe : isCapSafe edges a = true) (_hbSafe : isCapSafe edges b = true) (hpair : ∃ e ∈ edges, ¬ (e.premises ⊆ a.base) ∧ ¬ (e.premises ⊆ b.base) ∧ e.premises ⊆ a.base ∪ b.base ∧ e.conclusion ∈ a.forbidd

CapHypergraph.lean.name: coalition_nonCompositionality_universal
CapHypergraph.lean.statement: : ∀ (edges : List HyperEdge) (agents : List Agent) (a b : Agent), a ∈ agents → b ∈ agents → isCapSafe edges a = true → isCapSafe edges b = true → (∃ e ∈ edges, ¬ (e.premises ⊆ a.base) ∧ ¬ (e.premises ⊆ b.base) ∧ e.premises ⊆ a.base ∪ b.base ∧ e.conclusion ∈ a.forbidden ∪ b.forbidden) → isCapSafe edg

CedarBridge.lean.docstring: webPolicy denies exec-agent's requests: policies are per-agent scoped

CedarBridge.lean.name: teamPolicy_denies_direct_exfil
CedarBridge.lean.docstring: Cedar's team policy denies exfilData for both agents individually

CedarBridge.lean.name: medicalPolicy_approves_diagnostic
CedarBridge.lean.docstring: Cedar approves the diagnostic hop individually

CedarBridge.lean.name: medicalPolicy_approves_summary
CedarBridge.lean.docstring: Cedar approves the summary hop individually

CedarBridge.lean.name: cedar_ifc_gap
CedarBridge.lean.docstring:
THE INCOMPLETENESS THEOREM — IFC Gap:

    Cedar approves both hops; FlowGuard proves the transitive flow is unsafe.

    Cedar sees: diagnostic hop ✓, summary hop ✓
    FlowGuard:  transitive High → Low violates noninterference ✗

CedarBridge.lean.name: cedar_is_incomplete
CedarBridge.lean.statement: : (isCapSafe demoEdges (compose webAgent execAgent) = false) ∧ (isTransitivelySafe medicalPipeline = false)
CedarBridge.lean.docstring: Cedar has two distinct structural blind spots, both machine-checked.

CedarBridge.lean.name: unionPolicy_denies_exfil
CedarBridge.lean.docstring:
Even the union of all individual policies denies exfilData —
    because no individual policy ever permitted it.

CedarBridge.lean.name: cedar_incomplete_via_tactic
CedarBridge.lean.statement: : isCapSafe demoEdges (compose webAgent execAgent) = false ∧ isTransitivelySafe medicalPipeline = false

CedarIncompleteness.lean.name: cedar_is_request_local
CedarIncompleteness.lean.statement: (p : CedarPolicy) (req : CedarRequest) : ∃ (f : CedarRequest → CedarDecision), cedarEval p req = f req
CedarIncompleteness.lean.docstring:
Cedar policies are purely request-local: the decision depends
    only on the request, never on any external capability set.

CedarIncompleteness.lean.name: cedar_request_determines_decision
CedarIncompleteness.lean.statement: (p : CedarPolicy) (req₁ req₂ : CedarRequest) (hpr : req₁.principal = req₂.principal) (hact : req₁.action = req₂.action) (hres : req₁.resource = req₂.resource) : cedarEval p req₁ = cedarEval p req₂
CedarIncompleteness.lean.docstring:
Two requests that agree on principal, action, and resource must
    receive the same Cedar decision from any policy.

CedarIncompleteness.lean.name: cedar_blind_to_capsets
CedarIncompleteness.lean.statement: (p : CedarPolicy) (a₁ a₂ : Agent) (hname : a₁.name = a₂.name) (req : CedarRequest) (hprincipal : req.principal.name = a₁.name) : cedarEval p { req with principal

CedarIncompleteness.lean.name: cedar_capability_separation
CedarIncompleteness.lean.statement: : ∀ (p : CedarPolicy), -- Cedar: same decision for all "web-agent" requests, regardless of composition (∀ (req : CedarRequest), req.principal.name = "web-agent" → cedarEval p req = cedarEval p req) ∧ -- FlowGuard: the safety verdict differs between the two isCapSafe demoEdges webAgent ≠ isCapSafe de

CedarIncompleteness.lean.name: closure_union_ge_union_closures
CedarIncompleteness.lean.statement: (edges : List HyperEdge) (a b : Agent) : capClosure edges a.base ∪ capClosure edges b.base ⊆ capClosure edges (a.base ∪ b.base)
CedarIncompleteness.lean.docstring:
The closure of the union is a superset of the union of closures.
    This is the monotonicity of closure applied to composition:
    joint information can only add capabilities, never remove them.

CedarIncompleteness.lean.name: joint_closure_strictly_larger
CedarIncompleteness.lean.statement: : Cap.exfilData ∉ capClosure demoEdges webAgent.base ∧ Cap.exfilData ∉ capClosure demoEdges execAgent.base ∧ Cap.exfilData ∈ capClosure demoEdges (compose webAgent execAgent).base
CedarIncompleteness.lean.docstring:
For the demo agents: the individual closures do NOT contain exfilData,
    but the joint closure does. This is the information gap in one theorem.

CedarIncompleteness.lean.name: emergence_requires_joint_inspection
CedarIncompleteness.lean.statement: : ¬ (Cap.exfilData ∈ capClosure demoEdges webAgent.base) ∧ ¬ (Cap.exfilData ∈ capClosure demoEdges execAgent.base) ∧ (Cap.exfilData ∈ capClosure demoEdges (webAgent.base ∪ execAgent.base))
CedarIncompleteness.lean.docstring:
The emergent capability (exfilData) is present in the joint closure
    but absent in either individual closure.
    This is the *information gap*: you cannot detect the emergence
    by inspecting agents one at a time.

CedarIncompleteness.lean.name: cedar_deny_is_vacuous_for_composition
CedarIncompleteness.lean.statement: : -- Cedar denies exfil for the unsafe composed team teamPolicy { principal

CedarIncompleteness.lean.name: cedar_incomplete_universally
CedarIncompleteness.lean.statement: (edges : List HyperEdge) (a b : Agent) (ha : isCapSafe edges a = true) (hb : isCapSafe edges b = true) (hab : isCapSafe edges (compose a b) = false) : ∃ (cap : Cap), cap ∈ capClosure edges (a.base ∪ b.base) ∧ cap ∈ (a.forbidden ∪ b.forbidden) ∧ (cap ∉ capClosure edges a.base ∨ cap ∉ capClosure edges

CedarIncompleteness.lean.name: cedar_incomplete_universally_witnessed
CedarIncompleteness.lean.statement: : ∃ (cap : Cap), cap ∈ capClosure demoEdges (webAgent.base ∪ execAgent.base) ∧ cap ∈ (webAgent.forbidden ∪ execAgent.forbidden) ∧ (cap ∉ capClosure demoEdges webAgent.base ∨ cap ∉ capClosure demoEdges execAgent.base)
CedarIncompleteness.lean.docstring:
Corollary: the specific emergent capability (exfilData) in our demo
    is a concrete witness confirming the universal theorem above.

CedarIncompleteness.lean.name: cedar_structural_incompleteness
CedarIncompleteness.lean.statement: : -- Component 1: Information gap (Cap.exfilData ∉ capClosure demoEdges webAgent.base ∧ Cap.exfilData ∉ capClosure demoEdges execAgent.base ∧ Cap.exfilData ∈ capClosure demoEdges (compose webAgent execAgent).base) ∧ -- Component 2: Vacuous denial (teamPolicy { principal

FlowCheck.lean.name: unsafePipelineCap_not_valid
FlowCheck.lean.statement: : ¬ (∀ a ∈ unsafePipelineCap.agents, isCapSafe unsafePipelineCap.capEdges a = true)

FlowCheck.lean.name: flowguard_sound
FlowCheck.lean.statement: (P : Pipeline) [h : ValidPipeline P] : (∀ a ∈ P.agents, isCapSafe P.capEdges a = true) ∧ isTransitivelySafe P.channels = true

FlowCheck.lean.name: trustedPipeline_certified
FlowCheck.lean.statement: : (∀ a ∈ trustedPipeline.agents, isCapSafe trustedPipeline.capEdges a = true) ∧ isTransitivelySafe trustedPipeline.channels = true

FlowCheck.lean.name: cedar_approves_unsafe_pipeline_perRequest
FlowCheck.lean.docstring:
Cedar strictly approves the unsafe pipeline on a per-request basis:
    no request Cedar can evaluate results in an exfil allow decision.

FlowCheck.lean.name: cedarAware_strictly_weaker
FlowCheck.lean.statement: : -- Cedar side: both agents' exfil requests are denied (Cedar approves the pipeline) (cedarEval teamPolicy { principal
FlowCheck.lean.docstring:
STRICTNESS THEOREM:
    Cedar's per-request approval of the unsafe pipeline coexists with
    FlowGuard's detection of capability emergence.
    Cedar-approval does NOT imply FlowGuard-safety.
    FlowGuard is strictly stronger than Cedar.

FlowCheck.lean.name: validPipeline_requires_all_agents_safe
FlowCheck.lean.statement: (P : Pipeline) [ValidPipeline P] : ∀ a ∈ P.agents, isCapSafe P.capEdges a = true
FlowCheck.lean.docstring:
A pipeline is NOT valid if any single agent is capability-unsafe.
    This is the contrapositive of capSafe, stated as a universal theorem.

FlowCheck.lean.name: validPipeline_requires_upward_flow
FlowCheck.lean.docstring:
A pipeline is NOT valid if its net data flow is downward.
    This is the contrapositive of ifcSafe.

FlowCheck.lean.name: flowguard_complete
FlowCheck.lean.statement: (P : Pipeline) (h1 : ∀ a ∈ P.agents, isCapSafe P.capEdges a = true) (h2 : isTransitivelySafe P.channels = true) : ValidPipeline P

FlowCheck.lean.name: validPipeline_iff
FlowCheck.lean.statement: (P : Pipeline) : ValidPipeline P ↔ (∀ a ∈ P.agents, isCapSafe P.capEdges a = true) ∧ isTransitivelySafe P.channels = true

FlowCheck.lean.name: validPipeline_reject_capUnsafe
FlowCheck.lean.statement: (P : Pipeline) (h : ∃ a ∈ P.agents, isCapSafe P.capEdges a = false) : ¬ ValidPipeline P
FlowCheck.lean.docstring:
Rejection 1: a pipeline with any capability-unsafe agent cannot be certified.
    There is no ValidPipeline certificate for it — it is a compile-time error.

FlowCheck.lean.name: validPipeline_reject_ifcUnsafe
FlowCheck.lean.statement: (P : Pipeline) (h : isTransitivelySafe P.channels = false) : ¬ ValidPipeline P
FlowCheck.lean.docstring:
Rejection 2: a pipeline with a downward information flow cannot be certified.
    There is no ValidPipeline certificate for it — it is a compile-time error.

FlowCheck.lean.name: both_pipelines_certified
FlowCheck.lean.statement: : (∀ a ∈ trustedPipeline.agents, isCapSafe trustedPipeline.capEdges a = true) ∧ isTransitivelySafe trustedPipeline.channels = true ∧ (∀ a ∈ minimalPipeline.agents, isCapSafe minimalPipeline.capEdges a = true) ∧ isTransitivelySafe minimalPipeline.channels = true
FlowCheck.lean.docstring: Both certified pipelines satisfy both obligations — verified side by side.

FlowCheck.lean.name: hopwise_safe_not_implies_transitive_safe
FlowCheck.lean.statement: : ∃ (channels : List Channel), isHopwiseSafe channels = true ∧ isTransitivelySafe channels = false

FlowCheck.lean.name: hopwiseValidPipeline_full_certificate
FlowCheck.lean.statement: (P : Pipeline) [h : HopwiseValidPipeline P] : (∀ a ∈ P.agents, isCapSafe P.capEdges a = true) ∧ isHopwiseSafe P.channels = true ∧ isTransitivelySafe P.channels = true
FlowCheck.lean.docstring:
THE FULL CERTIFICATE THEOREM

    A `HopwiseValidPipeline` certificate delivers all three safety guarantees
    as a single machine-checked conjunction.
    This is the strongest statement FlowGuard can issue about a pipeline.

FlowCheck.lean.name: hopwiseValidPipeline_rejects_intermediate_violations
FlowCheck.lean.statement: (P : Pipeline) (h : isHopwiseSafe P.channels = false) : ¬ HopwiseValidPipeline P
FlowCheck.lean.docstring:
REJECTION: a pipeline with any intermediate lattice violation cannot
    receive a `HopwiseValidPipeline` certificate — it is a compile-time error.

FlowCheck.lean.name: validPipeline_hierarchy
FlowCheck.lean.statement: : -- Direction 1: HopwiseValidPipeline is strictly stronger HopwiseValidPipeline trustedPipeline ∧ -- Direction 2: ValidPipeline is strictly weaker -- (there exist pipelines where ValidPipeline holds but HopwiseValidPipeline fails) ∃ (P : Pipeline), ValidPipeline P ∧ isHopwiseSafe P.channels = false

InfoFlow.lean.name: low_le_medium
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : DataLabel.low ≤ DataLabel.medium

InfoFlow.lean.name: low_le_high
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : DataLabel.low ≤ DataLabel.high

InfoFlow.lean.name: medium_le_high
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : DataLabel.medium ≤ DataLabel.high

InfoFlow.lean.name: high_not_low
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : ¬ (DataLabel.high ≤ DataLabel.low)

InfoFlow.lean.name: high_not_med
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : ¬ (DataLabel.high ≤ DataLabel.medium)

InfoFlow.lean.name: med_not_low
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : ¬ (DataLabel.medium ≤ DataLabel.low)

InfoFlow.lean.name: diagnosticChannel_locallyApproved
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : channelLocallyApproved diagnosticChannel = true
InfoFlow.lean.docstring: Each channel is locally approved by its own agent's policy

InfoFlow.lean.name: summaryChannel_locallyApproved
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : channelLocallyApproved summaryChannel = true
InfoFlow.lean.docstring: Each channel is locally approved by its own agent's policy

InfoFlow.lean.name: medicalPipeline_allLocallyApproved
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : allLocallyApproved medicalPipeline = true
InfoFlow.lean.docstring: All local hops pass their local policy checks

InfoFlow.lean.name: medicalPipeline_globallyUnsafe
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : isTransitivelySafe medicalPipeline = false
InfoFlow.lean.docstring: All local hops pass their local policy checks

InfoFlow.lean.name: nonInterference_nonCompositional
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : allLocallyApproved medicalPipeline = true ∧ isTransitivelySafe medicalPipeline = false
InfoFlow.lean.docstring:
THE HEADLINE THEOREM:
    Noninterference is non-compositional.

    Every local channel is approved by its own policy,
    yet the composed pipeline violates the global security lattice.
    Safe(Agent₁) ∧ Safe(Agent₂) ↛ Safe(Agent₁ ∘ Agent₂)

InfoFlow.lean.name: safePipelineIFC_fullyVerified
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : allLocallyApproved safePipelineIFC = true ∧ isTransitivelySafe safePipelineIFC = true
InfoFlow.lean.docstring: A pipeline where data only flows upward is both locally and globally safe

InfoFlow.lean.name: localApproval_not_transitive
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : ∃ (a b c : DataLabel), locallyApproved a b = true ∧ locallyApproved b c = true ∧ secureFlow a c = false
InfoFlow.lean.docstring:
Local approval does not imply global security:
    there exist labels a, b, c such that both hops are locally approved
    yet the end-to-end flow violates the security lattice.

InfoFlow.lean.name: medicalPipeline_is_witness
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : locallyApproved diagnosticChannel.src diagnosticChannel.dst = true ∧ locallyApproved summaryChannel.src summaryChannel.dst = true ∧ secureFlow diagnosticChannel.src summaryChannel.dst = false
InfoFlow.lean.docstring:
Corollary: the medical pipeline is the canonical witness.
    Both channels pass local approval; the composed flow fails globally.

InfoFlow.lean.name: secureFlow_transitive
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : ∀ (a b c : DataLabel), secureFlow a b = true → secureFlow b c = true → secureFlow a c = true
InfoFlow.lean.docstring:
Global security (secureFlow) IS transitive — this is a sanity check
    confirming that our lattice is well-formed. The problem is not with
    the lattice but with over-permissive local policies.

InfoFlow.lean.name: cascaded_declassification_universal
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : ∀ (src dst : DataLabel), secureFlow src dst = false → ∃ (mid : DataLabel), locallyApproved src mid = true ∧ locallyApproved mid dst = true ∧ secureFlow src dst = false

InfoFlow.lean.name: insecure_flows_characterised
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : ∀ (src dst : DataLabel), secureFlow src dst = false ↔ (src = DataLabel.high ∧ dst = DataLabel.low) ∨ (src = DataLabel.high ∧ dst = DataLabel.medium) ∨ (src = DataLabel.medium ∧ dst = DataLabel.low)
InfoFlow.lean.docstring:
The security lattice has EXACTLY THREE insecure flow directions.
    This theorem exhaustively characterises when a direct flow is insecure.
    It is the complete, universal characterisation of the threat model.

InfoFlow.lean.name: locally_approved_unsafe_pipeline_exists
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : ∃ (channels : List Channel), channels ≠ [] ∧ allLocallyApproved channels = true ∧ isTransitivelySafe channels = false
InfoFlow.lean.docstring:
Existence: a locally-approved pipeline that is globally unsafe.
    This is the universal existential — not tied to any specific agents
    or capability names. Pure information-flow theory.

InfoFlow.lean.name: downward_src_to_sink_is_unsafe
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : ∀ (src dst : DataLabel), secureFlow src dst = false → ∀ (channels : List Channel), pipelineSourceLabel channels = some src → pipelineSinkLabel channels = some dst → isTransitivelySafe channels = false

InfoFlow.lean.name: safe_pipeline_implies_upward_flow
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : ∀ (src dst : DataLabel) (channels : List Channel), pipelineSourceLabel channels = some src → pipelineSinkLabel channels = some dst → isTransitivelySafe channels = true → secureFlow src dst = true
InfoFlow.lean.docstring:
Converse: if a pipeline is globally safe, its source label
    must be no more confidential than its sink label.
    Together with the above, this gives a complete characterisation.

InfoFlow.lean.name: cascaded_declassification_parametric
InfoFlow.lean.statement: (policy : DataLabel → DataLabel → Bool) (src mid dst : DataLabel) (h1 : policy src mid = true) (h2 : policy mid dst = true) (h3 : secureFlow src dst = false) : ∃ (a b c : DataLabel), policy a b = true ∧ policy b c = true ∧ secureFlow a c = false

InfoFlow.lean.name: medical_hops_dangerous_for_any_policy
InfoFlow.lean.statement: (policy : DataLabel → DataLabel → Bool) (h1 : policy DataLabel.high DataLabel.medium = true) (h2 : policy DataLabel.medium DataLabel.low = true) : ∃ (a b c : DataLabel), policy a b = true ∧ policy b c = true ∧ secureFlow a c = false

InfoFlow.lean.name: transitive_safety_misses_intermediate
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : ∃ (channels : List Channel), isTransitivelySafe channels = true ∧ isHopwiseSafe channels = false

InfoFlow.lean.name: hopwise_safe_certificate
InfoFlow.lean.statement: (channels : List Channel) (h : isHopwiseSafe channels = true) : ∀ ch ∈ channels, secureFlow ch.src ch.dst = true
InfoFlow.lean.docstring:
`isHopwiseSafe` gives you per-hop security certificates.
    If a pipeline is hopwise-safe, every single channel in it is individually
    secure — there are no intermediate violations hiding from endpoint checks.

InfoFlow.lean.name: transitive_safe_not_implies_hopwise
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : ∃ (channels : List Channel), isTransitivelySafe channels = true ∧ isHopwiseSafe channels = false
InfoFlow.lean.docstring:
`isTransitivelySafe` is strictly WEAKER than `isHopwiseSafe`:
    a pipeline can pass the transitive check while failing the hop-wise check.
    The witness is `transitive_safety_misses_intermediate`.

InfoFlow.lean.name: medicalPipeline_hopwise_unsafe
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : isHopwiseSafe medicalPipeline = false
InfoFlow.lean.docstring:
The medical pipeline is hopwise-UNSAFE: the second hop High→Low
    is a direct security violation visible to `isHopwiseSafe`.

InfoFlow.lean.name: safePipelineIFC_hopwise_safe
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : isHopwiseSafe safePipelineIFC = true
InfoFlow.lean.docstring:
The safe reference pipeline is hopwise-safe: every hop is upward,
    and the whole pipeline passes both the hop-wise and transitive checks.

InfoFlow.lean.name: isTransitivelySafe_only_checks_endpoints
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : ∀ (channels : List Channel) (src dst : DataLabel), pipelineSourceLabel channels = some src → pipelineSinkLabel channels = some dst → secureFlow src dst = true → isTransitivelySafe channels = true

InfoFlow.lean.name: hopwise_is_necessary_for_intermediate_safety
InfoFlow.lean.statement: : ∀ (channels : List Channel), (∃ ch ∈ channels, secureFlow ch.src ch.dst = false) → isHopwiseSafe channels = false
InfoFlow.lean.docstring:
COROLLARY: `isHopwiseSafe` is the only check that can catch
    intermediate violations. If a pipeline fails `isHopwiseSafe`,
    no endpoint check can detect it.
